item.description: The U.S. military shot down multiple incoming drones and struck one ground control station inside Iran, according to a U.S. official. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

media:content.media:title: U.S. military launches new strikes on Iranian drones
media:content.media:description: The U.S. military shot down multiple incoming drones and struck one ground control station inside Iran, according to a U.S. official. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

item.title: U.S. military launches new strikes on Iranian drones

item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa
Phil Helsel
Morgan Chesky
David Douglas
item.description: Washington was bracing for the state’s ‘deadliest industrial tragedy,’ Gov. Bob Ferguson said following the rupture of a tank at the Nippon Dynawave plant.

media:content.media:text: A sign for the main gate for the visitor center of Nippon Dynawave
media:content.media:description: Emergency responders from the Spill Response Team attend to the scene in front of the Nippon Dynawave Packaging facility following a fatal chemical storage tank implosion, in Longview, Wash., on May 26, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Washington State Chemical Plant Failure
media:content.media:description: Structural damage at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. in Longview, Wash., after a tank containing hazardous liquid imploded Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: A young woman places an electric candle down among dozens of others at a makeshift memorial.
media:content.media:description: A vigil at R.A. Long Park in Longview on Tuesday for victims of the storage tank failure.

item.title: 11 presumed dead in Washington state paper mill implosion

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
Matt Dixon
item.description: Ken Paxton has trailed Democratic state Rep. James Talarico in fundraising. Some Republicans say the party will have to spend millions to win the Senate race.

media:content.media:text: Ken Paxton.
media:content.media:description: Ken Paxton, Texas attorney general and Republican US Senate candidate, greets attendees during a Texas primary election night event in Dallas, Texas, US, on Tuesday, March 3, 2026. US Senator John Cornyn and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton are heading for a runoff in the Republican primary for the US Senate in Texas, according to projections by DDHQ. Photographer: Antranik Tavitian/Bloomberg via Getty Images

item.title: Republicans brace for money problems in Texas after Ken Paxton’s win

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
item.description: President Donald Trump went on a winning streak in May primaries as he ousted several Republicans who had dissented from his party line, further tightening his grip on the GOP.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Visits A Packaging Facility In Kentucky
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump (L) shakes hands with Republican congressional candidate for Kentucky, Ed Gallrein as they speak on stage at Verst Logistics on March 11, 2026 in Hebron, Kentucky.

item.title: Trump racks up May primary wins in Republican retribution campaign

item.description: A former CIA officer is accused of stashing more than 300 gold bars in his house, worth $40 million, as well as lying about his educational background. The man claimed he needed the gold bars for “work-related expenses,” according to court documents and two people familiar with his employment history. NBC News' Julia Ainsley breaks down the details in the exclusive story.

media:content.media:title: Former CIA officer accused of stashing 300 gold bars in his house
media:content.media:description: A former CIA officer is accused of stashing more than 300 gold bars in his house, worth $40 million, as well as lying about his educational background. The man claimed he needed the gold bars for “work-related expenses,” according to court documents and two people familiar with his employment history. NBC News' Julia Ainsley breaks down the details in the exclusive story.

item.title: Former CIA officer accused of stashing 300 gold bars in his house

item.description: In our series “The Cost of Denial,” NBC News’ Maggie Vespa reports on a 13-year-old Illinois girl who suffers from as many as four seizures a day. Insurance denied coverage for the surgery her doctor said could help, but then reversed its decision days after we asked about the case.

media:content.media:title: Insurance denies coverage for teen’s surgery to treat severe epilepsy
media:content.media:description: In our series “The Cost of Denial,” NBC News’ Maggie Vespa reports on a 13-year-old Illinois girl who suffers from as many as four seizures a day. Insurance denied coverage for the surgery her doctor said could help, but then reversed its decision days after we asked about the case.

item.title: Insurance denies coverage for teen’s surgery to treat severe epilepsy

item.dc:creator: Chloe Atkins
item.description: A Google software engineer was accused Wednesday of using confidential company information to make over $1.2 million on Polymarket in the second significant criminal case tied to trades on the prediction market platform.

media:content.media:text: A general exterior view of Google's headquarters.
media:content.media:description: A Google engineer is accused in the U.S. of using insider information to make more than $1 million off bets placed on Polymarket.

item.title: Google engineer made $1.2M placing bets on Polymarket using confidential info, prosecutors say

item.dc:creator: Mithil Aggarwal
Raf Sanchez
item.description: Their unlikely journey started dancing barefoot on a dusty street in 2014

media:content.media:text: Shakira invites Uganda's "Triplets Ghetto Kids" dance group to perform with her at the World Cup 2026 opening ceremony.
media:content.media:description: Shakira invites Uganda's "Triplets Ghetto Kids" dance group to perform with her at the World Cup 2026 opening ceremony.

media:content.media:text: The Ghetto Kids dance group reacts to a video message from Shakira.
media:content.media:description: The Ghetto Kids dance group reacts to a video message from Shakira.

media:content.media:text: The Ghetto Kids during an interview with NBC News.
media:content.media:description: The Ghetto Kids during an interview with NBC News.

media:content.media:text: Local residents celebrate the dance group's World Cup performance news.
media:content.media:description: Local residents celebrate the dance group's World Cup performance news.

item.title: Uganda’s Ghetto Kids will dance with Shakira at World Cup final halftime show

item.dc:creator: NBC News
item.description: Thursday is a decision day at the Supreme Court, which has yet to issue rulings on consequential cases like Trump v. Barbara on birthright citizenship or Trump v. Lisa Cook, the Federal Reserve official.

media:content.media:text: NBC News's Laura Jarrett Answers Your Questions
media:content.media:description: Laura Jarrett.

item.title: Send Laura Jarrett your questions about the Supreme Court and more

item.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

media:content.media:title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
media:content.media:description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

item.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio

item.dc:creator: Chloe Atkins
item.description: A Google software engineer was accused Wednesday of using confidential company information to make over $1.2 million on Polymarket in the second significant criminal case tied to trades on the prediction market platform.

media:content.media:text: A general exterior view of Google's headquarters.
media:content.media:description: A Google engineer is accused in the U.S. of using insider information to make more than $1 million off bets placed on Polymarket.

item.title: Google engineer made $1.2M placing bets on Polymarket using confidential info, prosecutors say

item.dc:creator: Tim Stelloh
item.description: A jury convicted an Arizona man Wednesday in the gunpoint robbery of an exotic dancer who was killed three years later in a murder plot that officials say he carried out with two other people.

media:content.media:text: A group of people standing in a circle holding candles in a downtown park at night.
media:content.media:description: Friends and family members gathered to remember Mercedes Vega at Rio Salado Park in Tempe, Ariz., in 2023.

media:content.media:text: Three photos of a young woman are hung with mini clothespins from a wire of LED lights on a wall.
media:content.media:description: Friends and family members gathered to remember Mercedes Vega at Rio Salado Park in Tempe, Ariz., in 2023.

item.title: Man charged in murder of Arizona dancer is found guilty of robbing her 3 years before she was killed

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
Matt Dixon
item.description: Ken Paxton has trailed Democratic state Rep. James Talarico in fundraising. Some Republicans say the party will have to spend millions to win the Senate race.

media:content.media:text: Ken Paxton.
media:content.media:description: Ken Paxton, Texas attorney general and Republican US Senate candidate, greets attendees during a Texas primary election night event in Dallas, Texas, US, on Tuesday, March 3, 2026. US Senator John Cornyn and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton are heading for a runoff in the Republican primary for the US Senate in Texas, according to projections by DDHQ. Photographer: Antranik Tavitian/Bloomberg via Getty Images

item.title: Republicans brace for money problems in Texas after Ken Paxton’s win

item.dc:creator: Courtney Kube
Jennifer Jett
item.description: Iran said Thursday that it had targeted a U.S. air base in response to U.S. military attacks in the south of the country, Reuters reported, citing Iranian state media

media:content.media:text: An aerial view of dozens of large ships anchored in a waterway in front of tan mountains.
media:content.media:description: Vessels anchored at the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam, Oman on May 25, 2026.

item.title: Iran claims attack on American air base after U.S. military strikes, further clouding peace deal prospects

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Maya Rosenberg
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard will reopen the search for Lynette Hooker, the Michigan woman who has been missing since April after she fell overboard in the Bahamas, according to a U.S. official familiar with the investigation

media:content.media:text: Lynette Hooker, missing woman who went overboard in the Bahamas.
media:content.media:description: Lynette Hooker.

media:content.media:text: U.S. Coast Guard Investigative Service officers seize the Soulmate, the boat Lynette and Brian Hooker were on the night she disappeared.
media:content.media:description: U.S. Coast Guard Investigative Service officers seize the Soulmate, the boat Lynette and Brian Hooker were on the night she disappeared.

media:content.media:text: The Soulmate, which was seized by the U.S. Coast Guard.
media:content.media:description: The Soulmate, which was seized by the U.S. Coast Guard.

item.title: Coast Guard plans new search in Bahamas for missing Michigan woman

item.dc:creator: Tim Stelloh
item.description: A baby gorilla born over the weekend at the Seattle zoo was delivered via a rare cesarean section, officials said

media:content.media:credit: Nichole Hamilton via Woodland Park Zoo
media:content.media:text: Three surgeons, one holding a newborn gorilla, in yellow surgical scrubs.
media:content.media:description: A medical team that typically works on humans helped deliver a baby gorilla via cesarean section at Woodland Park Zoo in Seattle on Sunday.

media:content.media:text: A gorilla holds onto a vine in a leafy landscape.
media:content.media:description: A medical team that typically works on humans helped deliver a baby gorilla via cesarean section at Woodland Park Zoo in Seattle on Sunday.

item.title: Baby gorilla delivered via rare C-section at Seattle zoo

item.dc:creator: Raquel Coronell Uribe
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden said in an interview clip released Wednesday that she thought her husband, former President Joe Biden, was “having a stroke” during his critical June 2024 debate, which catalyzed the chain of events that eventually led him to drop out of the presidential election.“I don’t know what happened.

media:content.media:text: US-POLITICS-ECONOMY-MILKEN
media:content.media:description: Former U.S. First Lady Dr. Jill Biden speaks during the 29th annual Milken Institute Global Conference at the Beverly Hilton in Beverly Hills, Calif., on May 5, 2026.

item.title: Jill Biden says she thought Joe Biden was ‘having a stroke’ during 2024 debate

item.dc:creator: Ramon Taylor
Marina Kopf
Maggie Vespa
item.description: Several weeks after NBC News reached out to Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield about the denial, the insurer changed its policy to include coverage of deep brain stimulation for certain children.

media:content.media:text: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: Cambrie Sharp sits on a trampoline, a dog sits on the lawn in front of her
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: “The medications that typically work for kids don’t work for her," Brandi Sharp said. "They cause a lot of rebounds."
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: Two hands are placed on top of denial letters
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: Brandi Sharp stands underneath a budding tree outside her home
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: Cambrie's doctors say deep brain stimulation could help reduce the frequency of her seizures.
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

media:content.media:text: Cambrie Sharp lays on a trampoline
media:content.media:description: Since she was 6 years old, Cambrie Sharp has had daily, uncontrolled seizures.

item.title: When drug after drug failed, a teen hoped brain surgery could fix her daily seizures. Insurance denied it.

item.dc:creator: Jared Perlo
item.description: The measure goes beyond legislation in California and New York regulating America’s largest AI companies. It still needs to be signed by the governor.

media:content.media:text: The exterior of the Illinois State Capitol building, featuring a statue of Abraham Lincoln at the steps leading to the entrance
media:content.media:description: The Illinois State Capitol in Springfield.

item.title: Illinois Legislature passes historic AI bill that would require third-party safety audits

item.dc:creator: The Politics Desk
item.description: In today’s edition, we take stock of President Donald Trump’s retribution campaign this month as Ken Paxton and James Talarico look ahead to the general election in Texas.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Holds Cabinet Meeting
media:content.media:description: Marco Rubio, U.S. secretary of state, from left, U.S. President Donald Trump, and Pete Hegseth, U.S. secretary of defense, during a cabinet meeting at the White House in Washington, DC, US, on Wednesday, May 27, 2026.

item.title: Trump’s May primary scorecard: From the Politics Desk

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: A New York lawmaker says state residents who tap the Trump administration’s controversial $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund should be required to pay a 100% state income tax on the money.

media:content.media:text: Alex Bores stands in a dimly lit room with purple and magenta colored lighting.
media:content.media:description: State Rep. Alex Bores, D-N.Y., a U.S. House candidate, at a town hall event in New York last month.

media:content.media:text: A blue banner with Donald Trump's face hanging on the outside of the DOJ headquarters.
media:content.media:description: State Rep. Alex Bores, D-N.Y., a U.S. House candidate, at a town hall event in New York last month.

item.title: Proposed bill would tax New Yorkers who tap ‘anti-weaponization’ fund at 100%

item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa
Phil Helsel
Morgan Chesky
David Douglas
item.description: Washington was bracing for the state’s ‘deadliest industrial tragedy,’ Gov. Bob Ferguson said following the rupture of a tank at the Nippon Dynawave plant.

media:content.media:text: A sign for the main gate for the visitor center of Nippon Dynawave
media:content.media:description: Emergency responders from the Spill Response Team attend to the scene in front of the Nippon Dynawave Packaging facility following a fatal chemical storage tank implosion, in Longview, Wash., on May 26, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Washington State Chemical Plant Failure
media:content.media:description: Structural damage at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. in Longview, Wash., after a tank containing hazardous liquid imploded Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: A young woman places an electric candle down among dozens of others at a makeshift memorial.
media:content.media:description: A vigil at R.A. Long Park in Longview on Tuesday for victims of the storage tank failure.

item.title: 11 presumed dead in Washington state paper mill implosion

item.dc:creator: Julia Ainsley
Dan De Luce
Gary Grumbach
item.description: A former CIA senior officer with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing 300 gold bars that he said he needed for “work-related expenses,” sources say.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of a Department of Justice seal, left, and gold bars

item.title: Former CIA officer accused of stealing 300 gold bars, sources say

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
Tim Stelloh
item.description: The death of a Southern California man who was attacked outside his home decorated with Donald Trump memorabilia has left his family struggling to cope.

media:content.media:text: Kerry Sheron, right, a Southern California man known for his patriotic and pro-Trump home decorations died after he was assaulted outside his house, authorities said.
media:content.media:description: Kerry Sheron, right, a Southern California man known for his patriotic and pro-Trump home decorations, died after he was assaulted outside his house, authorities said.

item.title: Man known for pro-Trump displays who died after attack outside his home was ‘caring’ and ‘kind,’ family says
